discovery_instructions: Identify what the main item/entity is for this feature (e.g., employee record, document, invitation)
Look for list/table views - the rows represent the primary entity
Check page headers and breadcrumbs for entity naming
Note the singular and plural forms used in the UI

find_trigger: Look for buttons with: + Add, + Create, + New, plus icon
Check top-right corner of the page
Look in empty state messages for create prompts
create.validation: New item appears in list with unique identifier

find_trigger: Click on any row in a list/table
Look for 'View' or eye icon buttons
Check if rows are clickable (cursor changes)
read.validation: Detail panel, modal, or page opens showing full information

find_trigger: Look for Edit button, pencil icon
Check for inline editing (click on field to edit)
Look for status change dropdowns or buttons
Check action menus (three-dot menu) for edit option
update.validation: Changes persist after save, reflected in list view

find_trigger: Look for Delete button, trash icon
Check action menus (three-dot menu) for delete option
Look for bulk delete with checkboxes
Some features use 'Archive' or 'Cancel' instead of delete
delete.validation: Item removed from list or status changed to deleted/archived

instructions: Navigate to the feature's main section using the navigation_map
Take a full page snapshot to understand the layout
Find ALL clickable elements: buttons, links, tabs, dropdowns, icons
Map the navigation structure: tabs, sidebar items, breadcrumbs
Click each tab/sub-section and document what appears
Locate CRUD controls for the primary entity
Check for action menus (three-dot menus, right-click menus)
Document any modals, dialogs, slide-out panels
Identify form fields and input types when forms appear
Look for filters, search, sort, and export options
Check for bulk action controls (checkboxes, select all)
Note any settings or configuration options
discovery_targets: List/table of items with row actions

content_clusters.consolidation_notes: Merged payroll file templates, leave encashment configuration, and multi-currency setup. PRDs enrich with currency validation rules and payroll table redesign details.
Article covers unpaid leave deduction configuration. PRDs add automated deduction creation from Time Off approvals, overtime calculations, and cross-module integration architecture.
Article covers viewing payroll history and downloading payslips. PRD adds detailed pay breakdown dialog and multi-currency payslip generation.
PRD-only cluster covering overtime policy configuration, Excel template download/upload, and automatic amount calculation for payroll integration.
Grouped tickets affecting leave request submission, approval notifications, and date handling constraints. All impact employee and manager workflows during leave management.
Grouped tickets affecting leave type setup, editing, and configuration constraints. All impact HR Admin ability to maintain flexible leave policies.
Grouped tickets affecting attendance and leave report visibility, filtering, and historical data display. All impact HR Admin and People Manager reporting capabilities.
Grouped tickets affecting attendance status editing, concurrent edit handling, and missing configuration resolution. All impact day-to-day attendance management.
Grouped tickets affecting UI components, dialogs, tooltips, and calendar workflows. All impact user experience across multiple modules.
Single high-severity ticket affecting unpaid leave with payroll deductions. Impacts Finance Manager and HR Admin workflows.
Grouped tickets affecting attendance policy visibility, push notifications, and trust domain configurations. Impact setup and employee communication.

product_context.title: PRD: Multiple currency with new payroll table and the submit flow
product_context.why_built: UAE companies with overseas employees (sales teams abroad, remote hires) currently manage non-AED payroll in Excel, causing human errors, lack of system of record, and unmanaged audit trails. This is the most requested payroll feature per sales reports. Enables companies to manage payroll data and expenses for employees earning in non-AED currencies within the same system, eliminating parallel Excel processes.
key_design_decisions: Currency field added to salary configuration with searchable dropdown (default AED, supports all currencies)
System validates no paid/unpaid items exist before allowing currency change to prevent data inconsistency
Payroll table redesigned with currency tabs for multi-currency view and management
Currency-specific transaction submission in payroll submit flow
Auto-conversion of unpaid items at currency update removes manual validations
Non-AED employees cannot be added to onshore trade license - validation enforced in multiple places
Payslips generated in employee's base currency

product_context.title: PRD: Facilitating payroll tools and
product_context.why_built: Finance managers struggle with payroll table usability, need efficient employee selection for payment, quick identification of missing information, and detailed pay breakdowns. Core JTBD: Enable finance managers to efficiently select what and who to pay when submitting payroll, quickly identify and fix employees with missing information, view detailed pay breakdowns, and generate payslips. Support multi-currency payroll management for non-AED employees.
key_design_decisions: Payroll table redesigned with no horizontal scroll for 1440px+ width, consolidated columns (Basic+Allowances, Variable Pay, Gross Monthly, Net Adjustments, Work Expenses)
Click employee row to view detailed pay breakdown dialog with all salary components
Filters to find employees with missing information (country of residence, trade license, IBAN, MOL ID, UAE Exchange ID)
Complete missing information through popup dialog without leaving payroll table
Navigate between employees using previous/next while maintaining filters
Upload variable pay via Excel sheet with overflow menu access
Select employees and currencies for payroll submission in single flow
Generate and download payslips (individual or all) from closed month view
Payslip includes message: 'Computer-generated, does not need signature'

product_context.title: PRD: Unpaid leave deductions
product_context.why_built: Automate unpaid leave deductions by integrating Time Off module with Payroll module. Eliminate manual data transfer between modules when employees take unpaid leave, automatically calculating and applying salary deductions based on approved unpaid leave requests.
key_design_decisions: Upon full approval of unpaid leave request, system automatically creates payroll deduction
Deduction appears in payroll adjustments with calculated amount
Payroll processor receives email notification of new deduction
For multi-month leave, deductions are split across relevant payroll months
Future month deductions scheduled but email delayed until month opens
Closed payroll months cannot receive new deductions - handled via email notification
Deductions are non-editable to maintain data consistency
Overlapping deductions show remarks similar to recurring deductions

product_context.title: PRD: Overtime calculations on payroll
product_context.why_built: Enable admins to configure overtime policies and upload overtime hours for payroll calculations. Admins create named overtime policies with rate formulas, download Excel templates with employee data and calculated rates, fill in overtime hours offline, and upload back for automatic amount calculation and payroll integration.
key_design_decisions: Named overtime policies with configurable pay items (basic salary/allowances) and hourly rate multiplier
Hourly rate formula: multiplier × ((basic salary + allowances) / calendar days) / working hours
Excel template download with auto-populated employee data and pre-calculated rates
Offline Excel entry for overtime hours per employee
Mass upload overtime via 3-dot menu on payroll table
Automatic amount calculation (rate × hours) upon upload
Amounts flow into payroll calculations automatically
Future integration potential with Attendance module for automated hour tracking

product_context.title: PRD: Allowing existing lulu customers to process payroll through Bayzat (draft)
product_context.why_built: Enable companies already processing payroll with Lulu Exchange to use Bayzat platform with proper processing fee handling, and start charging clients after their first free year of payroll processing ends.
key_design_decisions: Processing fees toggle in admin panel for enabling/disabling charges
Two client types: Direct Lulu client (custom fees) and Bayzat client (pre-agreed fees)
Direct clients enter custom fees (bank transfer fee, Lulu open loop card fee)
Bayzat clients see pre-agreed amounts (editable with comment required)
Free year clients see crossed-out amounts (savings display only, not in calculations)
Currency is AED by default
Processing fee visible per transaction for funding Lulu account
Edit requires comment stating reason for audit trail

detected_integrations.description: Unpaid leave requests and approvals trigger automatic payroll deductions. Leave policy configuration (calendar vs working days) determines deduction calculation method. Comp-off/days in lieu impact leave balances.

Attendance data (check-in/check-out, overtime hours) feeds into payroll calculations. Overtime records and violations automatically adjust payroll. Future integration for automated overtime hour tracking from attendance system.
Wire transfer processing for payroll distribution. Bayzat tracks transactions and processing fees. Companies fund Lulu account for employee salary distribution.
Corporate banking systems used for wire transfers to Lulu Exchange. Bank transfer fees tracked for processing cost calculations.
Payroll file templates support DEWS and other government reporting requirements. Trade license and MOL data integrated for compliance.
Custom fields can be created in payroll file templates when standard fields are insufficient for specific reporting or processing needs.
Employee salary data, identifiers, and personal information used for payroll calculations. Pay breakdown dialog links to profile for editing missing information.
Work expenses and mileage reimbursements tracked and processed through payroll. Approved expenses must be unpaid before currency changes.
Leave request creation, approval workflows, and leave type configuration directly impact attendance reporting and employee leave balance visibility.
Unpaid leave with payroll deductions requires integration with payroll calculation engine for accurate salary reduction and compensation tracking.
Attendance status editing, concurrent edit handling, and policy enforcement depend on core attendance data management.
Leave request approval notifications and attendance policy notifications require integration with push notification service and navigation routing.
Offboarded employee filtering in attendance reports requires integration with employee lifecycle management to prevent stale records.
Custom attendance and leave reports require flexible filtering, data aggregation, and role-based view configuration.

check_instructions: Navigate to Automations in the left sidebar
Click + Create workflow (or similar create button)
In trigger selection, search for this feature's name
Check if this feature appears as a trigger source
Document any trigger events found (e.g., 'item created', 'status changed')
Check Actions section for actions related to this feature
Look for 'Approval' or 'Pending Approval' status in the feature
Check if items have approval workflow indicators
Look for 'Submit for Approval' buttons or actions
Find approval settings in feature configuration
Document the approval chain (who approves what)
Test approval/rejection flow if test data available
Check for approval history or audit trail

what_to_do.task: Configure Payroll File Templates for Custom Processing
steps: Navigate to Settings > Payroll tab > Payroll File Templates
Click 'Create New Template' and select template type (payroll processing or reporting)
Choose to use pre-defined template or upload existing file
Define template details: enter SIF template name, specify payment method, add bank name, include trade license
Map data fields: rename columns, move columns, delete unnecessary columns, map system data points to file columns
Create custom fields if required data is not available in standard fields
Review template configuration and save
what_to_do.expected_outcome: Custom payroll file template created and ready for use in payroll processing, reducing manual data entry errors and enabling efficient file generation for banks and government entities
rules_and_constraints: Must select either payroll processing or non-payroll processing template type
Can select pre-defined templates or upload existing files
Data field mapping must be completed correctly before saving
Custom fields can be created if standard fields are insufficient
Template must include all required fields for target system (bank, government)
edge_cases: Potential for clerical errors if manual work is done in payroll Excel files - mitigate by using templates
Careful data field mapping required to prevent misalignment with target systems
Custom field creation needed when standard fields don't cover all required data points

what_to_do.task: Configure Leave Encashment for End-of-Service Calculations
steps: Navigate to Settings > Payroll tab > End of Service Eligibility section
Click 'Configure' to access leave encashment settings
Select salary components for calculation: choose between Basic Only or Basic + Allowances
Select calculation method: Calendar Days, Working Days, or Custom Days
Configure individual leave types eligible for end-of-service encashment
Review which existing paid leave-type policies will be overwritten
Save configuration
what_to_do.expected_outcome: Leave encashment policy configured for end-of-service calculations, ensuring employees receive accurate financial compensation for unused leave upon separation
rules_and_constraints: Leave types must be pre-configured in the system before selection
Can choose between Basic Salary or Basic Salary + Allowances for calculation basis
Daily rate calculation uses Calendar or Working days based on Time Off policy configuration
Calendar/Working day calculation method is determined by existing Time Off policy and cannot be changed in this screen
Can select different calculation methods for different leave types
edge_cases: Super admins can see which eligible paid leave-type policies will be overwritten by new configuration
Existing Time Off policy settings determine day calculation type - cannot override here

what_to_do.task: Download and Export Payroll Table Data
what_to_do.persona: HR Admin | Finance Manager | Super Admin
steps: Navigate to Payroll Table section
Review payroll data in consolidated table view (Basic+Allowances, Variable Pay, Gross Monthly, Net Adjustments, Work Expenses columns)
Click 'Download' button to export payroll table
Optionally select or change payroll month before download
Confirm Excel file download
Open downloaded file for record-keeping, analysis, or submission to external parties
what_to_do.expected_outcome: Payroll table exported to Excel format for external reporting, record-keeping, bank submissions, and government compliance documentation
rules_and_constraints: Can download payroll table before or after closing the monthly pay run
Can download payroll tables for current and previous months from different tabs
Table format displays no horizontal scroll for 1440px width and above
Minimum 8 employee rows visible in table view
edge_cases: Can download from different tabs depending on payroll month status (current vs closed)
Multi-currency payroll displays currency tabs - select appropriate currency before download

source_articles.title: How to download the payroll table

what_to_do.task: Process Payroll via Wire Transfer (Lulu Exchange)
what_to_do.persona: Finance Manager | HR Admin | Super Admin
steps: Log into corporate banking system
Add Lulu Exchange as a beneficiary in banking system
Calculate total payroll amount required
Initiate wire transfer to Lulu Exchange before 12:00 pm for same-day processing
Obtain MPN (Money Processing Number) from wire transfer confirmation
Return to Bayzat Payroll Transactions page
Click 'Confirm Funds Released' button
Input transaction details (MPN number, transfer amount, date)
Submit payment confirmation
Monitor for Central Bank of UAE processing delays if applicable
what_to_do.expected_outcome: Payroll funds successfully transferred to Lulu Exchange for employee salary distribution, with transaction confirmed in Bayzat system for audit trail and reconciliation
rules_and_constraints: Funds must be transferred before 12:00 pm to ensure same-day salary processing
Transfer subject to potential Central Bank of UAE delays
MPN number required for transaction confirmation
Wire transfer must be to Lulu Exchange as designated beneficiary
Transaction details must be accurately input for reconciliation
edge_cases: Recommended to verify fund transfer by end of day before intended payment date
Potential delays from Central Bank of UAE may affect same-day processing
Multiple wire transfers may be needed for multi-currency payroll

steps: Navigate to Settings > Leaves > Leave Policies
Click 'Add New Policy' to create a new leave policy
Select 'Unpaid Leave' option for leave type
Choose salary calculation method: Basic Only or Basic + Allowances
Select day calculation type: Calendar Days, Working Days, or Custom Days
Configure policy name and other details
Save the unpaid leave policy
what_to_do.expected_outcome: Unpaid leave deduction policy configured and ready to automatically calculate daily salary deductions when employees take unpaid leave
rules_and_constraints: Unpaid leave deductions must be calculated daily and set off against employee's monthly pay
Can choose between Basic Only or Basic + Allowances for daily rate calculation
Can select Calendar Days, Working Days, or Custom Days for calculation basis
Daily pay formula: Salary Amount / Total days (calendar or working based on policy)
Salary = Basic + Allowances (if selected)
edge_cases: Multiple options for daily rate calculation - Basic Only vs Basic + Allowances affects deduction amount
Day calculation type must align with company's Time Off policy settings

what_to_do.task: Process Automated Unpaid Leave Deductions from Time Off Approvals
what_to_do.persona: HR Admin | Finance Manager | Super Admin
steps: Employee or admin submits unpaid leave request in Time Off module
Leave request goes through manager and HR approval workflow
Upon final approval, system automatically creates payroll deduction
Deduction appears in Payroll > Adjustments with calculated amount
Review deduction details and remarks (similar to recurring deductions)
For multi-month leave, system automatically splits deductions across relevant payroll months
Receive email notification when deduction is created
Include deduction in payroll run processing
Deduction is locked and non-editable to maintain data consistency
what_to_do.expected_outcome: Unpaid leave deductions automatically calculated and applied to payroll based on approved leave requests, eliminating manual data transfer between Time Off and Payroll modules
rules_and_constraints: Deduction cannot be edited once created - must adjust leave request to recalculate
Cannot create deductions for closed payroll months - handled via email notification
Multi-month leave creates separate deductions per calendar month
Daily pay formula: (Basic + Allowances) / Calendar days × Leave days
Deductions are non-editable to maintain data consistency
Future month deductions scheduled but email delayed until month opens
edge_cases: Leave spanning multiple months requires split deductions per month
Closed payroll months cannot receive new deductions - notification sent instead
Overlapping deductions show remarks similar to recurring deductions
Approved expense requests must be unpaid before currency change affects deduction

what_to_do.task: View Employee Payroll History and Download Payslips
steps: Navigate to individual employee's profile
Select 'Pay History' tab
Review list of previous monthly payroll payments
Locate desired payslip in the list
Click download icon next to payslip to download individual payslip
Open downloaded payslip file (PDF format)
Review payslip details including salary components, deductions, and net pay
what_to_do.expected_outcome: Employee or manager can access and download historical payslips for record-keeping, verification, or personal financial management
rules_and_constraints: Payslips can only be downloaded after being generated for a closed payrun
Payslips must first be generated on Payroll Transactions tab before they can be viewed or downloaded
Payslips include message: 'Computer-generated, does not need signature'
Multi-currency payslips generated in employee's base currency
edge_cases: Payslips only available after payrun is closed and payslips are generated
Employees can only view their own payslips; managers can view team payslips

source_articles.title: How to view employee payroll history of salary payments and payslips

what_to_do.task: Configure and Upload Overtime Policies for Payroll Calculation
steps: Navigate to Payroll configuration section > Overtime subsection
Click 'Add New' to create new overtime policy
Name the overtime policy (e.g., 'Weekend Overtime', 'Holiday Overtime')
Configure pay items included in hourly rate calculation (Basic Salary and/or Allowances)
Set hourly rate multiplier (e.g., 1.5x, 2x for premium rates)
Save policy configuration
Download Excel template with employee data and pre-calculated hourly rates
Review pre-populated employee identifiers, names, and calculated rates
Fill in overtime hours per employee in the Excel sheet offline
Return to Payroll table and access 3-dot menu > 'Mass Upload Overtime'
Upload completed Excel sheet
Confirm ready to proceed with overtime data
System auto-calculates amounts (rate × hours) and applies to payroll
what_to_do.expected_outcome: Overtime policies configured and overtime hours uploaded for automatic payroll calculation, eliminating manual overtime amount entry and reducing calculation errors
rules_and_constraints: Hourly rate formula: Rate multiplier × ((basic salary + allowances) / calendar days) / working hours
Excel sheet auto-populates: employee identifier, first name, last name, rate per hour
Overtime hours left blank in template for admin to fill
Amount auto-calculated from rate × hours
Each overtime policy appears as column in Excel
Employee identifier sourced from Work section profile
Must setup overtime rate multipliers in configuration before downloading Excel
edge_cases: Overtime hours uploaded before policy configuration complete - will fail validation
Employee identifier empty in profile - prevents correct rate calculation
Salary or allowances not set - rate calculation fails
Excel uploaded with wrong format - upload rejected
Overtime hours negative or excessive - validation warning
Policy deleted after hours already uploaded - orphaned data handling needed
Multiple policies with overlapping employees - clear column mapping required

steps: Identify desired payroll cycle start date (e.g., 15th of month)
Identify desired payroll cycle end date (e.g., 14th of next month)
Contact your assigned Customer Success Manager
Communicate new payroll cycle date range
Provide business justification for custom cycle (e.g., financial year alignment)
Confirm configuration details with Customer Success Manager
Receive confirmation of payroll cycle implementation
Verify payroll cycle implementation in system
what_to_do.expected_outcome: Custom payroll cycle dates configured to align with organization's specific financial cycles instead of default calendar month (1st to last)
rules_and_constraints: Payroll cycle configuration must be done through Customer Success Manager from backend
Cannot be self-configured by user through UI
Requires careful consideration when transitioning from static dates to custom cycle
Configuration affects all subsequent payroll processing
edge_cases: Transitioning from static dates (1st to last of month) to custom cycle requires careful planning
Existing payroll data may need adjustment during transition

what_to_watch_out_for.limitation: Employees cannot submit leave requests for historical dates or dates outside predefined leave cycles. The system restricts retrospective leave entry and lacks flexible date handling for non-standard periods, preventing legitimate leave documentation.

Leave request approval push notifications do not navigate users to the relevant leave request screen, and the attendance table does not auto-refresh after leave request creation. Users must manually reload pages to see updated information, creating inconsistent approval workflows.

HR Admins cannot modify leave type configurations after initial setup, including changing between paid/unpaid status, updating days allocation, or editing leave types in limited mode. The system enforces rigid leave type definitions that cannot adapt to changing organizational needs.

Attendance and leave reports lack comprehensive filtering capabilities, custom reporting views, and historical data visibility. Leave balance columns disappear when filters are applied, and offboarded employees continue appearing in reports. The system cannot provide role-based, customizable reporting for admins and managers.

what_to_watch_out_for.theme: Attendance Status Editing & Concurrent Edit Handling
what_to_watch_out_for.limitation: Admins and line managers cannot edit daily attendance status, and the system experiences errors during concurrent edits of attendance times. Additionally, the system cannot handle employees with missing office or scheduling configurations, preventing seamless attendance record updates.

Multiple UI components exhibit interaction issues: payroll attachment deletion is incomplete, attendance policy dialogs lack proper visibility and exit mechanisms, tooltips persist inappropriately during scrolling, and the admin calendar does not clearly separate viewing from approval actions. These defects create friction in daily workflows.

what_to_watch_out_for.theme: Unpaid Leave with Payroll Deduction Calculations
what_to_watch_out_for.limitation: The platform lacks robust mechanisms for handling unpaid leave with precise payroll deduction calculations. The system cannot automatically manage complex leave scenarios involving salary reductions and compensation tracking, limiting flexibility in leave policy implementation.

The platform lacks visibility of attendance policy tabs for employees, preventing meaningful push notifications about policy processes. Additionally, attendance tracking functionality for trust domain configurations is incomplete, limiting comprehensive attendance verification and monitoring.

persona_coverage.coverage_notes: All personas have reported issues. HR Admin has highest issue count (6), reflecting their role in configuration and reporting. Finance Manager has minimal coverage (1 issue) focused on payroll integration.

uncovered_nav_entries.label: Payroll
uncovered_nav_entries.purpose: Overtime hours feed into payroll calculations

execution_order: 2. EXPLORATION - Navigate to feature, discover all UI elements using exploration_phase instructions
3. IDENTIFY ENTITY - Determine the primary item/entity using primary_entity discovery
4. CRUD TEST - Test create, read, update, delete for the primary entity
5. WORKFLOW CHECK - Verify if feature has workflow triggers/actions
6. APPROVAL FLOW - Test approval process if present
7. WHAT_TO_DO - Execute each task from what_to_do array, validate expected outcomes
8. WHAT_TO_WATCH_OUT_FOR - Validate known limitations and edge cases
9. DOCUMENTATION - Capture screenshots of every significant state change
10. REPORT - Generate validation report with pass/fail for each task and issue
